spec.request_text:
Build a small Python habit tracker library and CLI without external dependencies.

Required behavior:
- Create a habit_tracker.py module.
- Define a Habit dataclass with id, name, cadence, created_at, and completed_dates fields.
- Define an in-memory HabitStore class with add, list_all, complete, streak, and delete methods.
- Cadence must support "daily" and "weekly"; invalid cadence should raise ValueError.
- Completing the same habit for the same date should be idempotent.
- Streak should count consecutive daily completions ending at a supplied date.
- Create a tiny argparse CLI in habit_tracker_cli.py for add, list, complete, streak, and delete.
- Add focused pytest tests for add/list, invalid cadence, idempotent completion, daily streaks, delete, and CLI smoke behavior.
- Add a short README.md with usage examples.

Planning requirement:
- Split the implementation into small autonomous tasks:
  1. core Habit model and store behavior
  2. streak and validation edge cases
  3. CLI wrapper and README
  4. pytest coverage
- Keep every task independently testable.

Constraints:
- Use only the Python standard library.
- Do not create a web app.
- Do not add dependencies.
- Tests must pass with pytest.
spec.title: habit-tracker-planfirst-smoke-20260704

pm.title: Python Habit Tracker Library and CLI
pm.problem_statement: Users need a lightweight, dependency-free way to track daily and weekly habits with streak counting and CLI access.
small_parts: Define Habit dataclass with id, name, cadence, created_at, completed_dates
Create CLI with argparse for add/list/complete/streak/delete
Write pytest tests for all core behaviors
acceptance_tests: Habit dataclass stores all required fields
HabitStore.add raises ValueError for invalid cadence
HabitStore.streak returns correct count for consecutive days
pytest passes for all test cases
open_questions: Should streak reset on missed days or only on explicit delete?
What date format should CLI accept?

architect.summary: Architecture for a dependency-free Python habit tracker library and CLI with in-memory storage, supporting daily/weekly cadences, streak counting, and CLI commands.
components: habit_tracker.py: Core library with Habit dataclass and HabitStore class
habit_tracker_cli.py: CLI interface using argparse for add, list, complete, streak, delete
tests/: Pytest test suite covering core logic and CLI smoke tests
data_flows: CLI receives command and arguments, instantiates HabitStore, delegates to store methods, formats and prints output
HabitStore maintains a list of Habit dataclasses, handling validation, idempotency, and streak calculation in memory
Tests import habit_tracker and habit_tracker_cli to verify behavior
risks: Incorrect streak calculation for weekly cadence or missed days
CLI argument conflicts or missing required arguments
Date format inconsistencies between CLI and store
decisions: Use Python standard library only (dataclasses, datetime, argparse, pytest)
In-memory storage via list of dataclasses for simplicity and testability
Strict cadence validation (daily, weekly) with ValueError
Idempotent completion by checking existing dates in completed_dates
Streak calculation based on consecutive days ending at a supplied date

planner.goal: Build a dependency-free Python habit tracker library and CLI with in-memory storage, supporting daily/weekly cadences, streak counting, idempotent completion, and comprehensive tests

tasks.title: Create Habit dataclass
tasks.description: Define Habit dataclass with id, name, cadence, created_at, and completed_dates fields. Validate cadence is 'daily' or 'weekly' in __post_init__.
acceptance_criteria: Habit dataclass exists with all required fields

tasks.title: Implement add and list_all methods
tasks.description: Implement HabitStore.add() to store habits and HabitStore.list_all() to return all stored habits as a list.

tasks.title: Implement complete method with idempotency
tasks.description: Implement HabitStore.complete() that marks a habit as completed for a given date. Same date completion should be idempotent.
acceptance_criteria: Same date completion does not duplicate
Raises ValueError for unknown habit id

tasks.title: Implement streak calculation
tasks.description: Implement HabitStore.streak() that counts consecutive daily completions ending at a supplied date.

tasks.title: Implement delete method
tasks.description: Implement HabitStore.delete() to remove a habit by id. Raises ValueError if habit not found.
acceptance_criteria: delete() raises ValueError for unknown id

tasks.title: Create CLI with argparse
tasks.description: Create habit_tracker_cli.py with argparse subcommands: add, list, complete, streak, delete. Each command calls corresponding store method.

tasks.title: Write pytest tests for core library
tasks.description: Create test_habit_tracker.py with tests for: add/list, invalid cadence, idempotent completion, daily streaks, delete.

tasks.title: Write pytest tests for CLI
tasks.description: Create test_cli.py with tests for CLI smoke behavior: verify each command runs without error and produces expected output.

tasks.title: Write README.md
tasks.description: Create README.md with usage examples showing how to use the library and CLI for adding, listing, completing, checking streaks, and deleting habits.

notes: All 9 small parts covered with implementation or test_writer tasks
Dependencies form a valid DAG with no cycles
Each task has narrow, testable acceptance criteria
Tests depend on implementation tasks they validate
README is last to avoid blocking core behavior
